blocks.content: The coast had a particular kind of quiet. Salt air and something older underneath — the smell of places that have been weathered into themselves over a long time.
Mara had handled the booking. I hadn't looked at the listing. I had looked at the satellite image, once, and then closed the tab. Force of habit.

Our rental was a grey-and-white structure on a modest lot. Clean lines. Good sight lines from the porch. Forty feet to the left was a larger house — two stories, wraparound porch, six vehicles parked at angles on a gravel pad.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I'm saying it out loud because awareness hasn't historically been enough.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I know how to take a vacation.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: No behavioral analysis. No reading people. No going completely still when someone says something and you're already three layers deep into what it means.

options.label: "I don't know how to turn it off."
